source: 芯片与存储板块投资研究报告.docx
agent_instruction: Use as research context only. Do not treat as a trade instruction. Verify live price, valuation, trend, beta, alpha contribution, and thesis invalidation before alerts.
core_thesis: AI data center demand, HBM, enterprise SSD, advanced process, and advanced packaging remain the main semiconductor/memory drivers.
Memory strength is not uniform: high-end AI/HBM/enterprise storage is stronger than consumer PC/phone-driven memory.
The sector has already rallied hard, so risk is now high expectations plus high beta plus high volatility.
Preferred structure is core growth exposure plus tactical high-beta memory names, not all-in chasing the hottest stocks.

preferred_core_watchlist.why: Advanced foundry quality anchor; AI chip manufacturing and advanced packaging exposure.
DRAM/NAND/HBM/enterprise SSD direct memory cycle exposure.
AI storage infrastructure and data center storage exposure with more defensive character than small high-beta names.
Memory interface / DDR5 / CXL / Retimer exposure, high margin but high valuation.
NOR/NAND/MCU platform, China replacement theme, high valuation already reflects optimism.
China foundry scarcity/policy exposure; useful as defensive local semiconductor allocation, not a chase-heavy position.

tactical_high_beta_watchlist.why: BIWIN storage module / enterprise SSD / AI edge elasticity; high cash-flow and leverage risk.
Longsys storage products and controller/firmware/manufacturing integration; high earnings elasticity and balance-sheet risk.
NAND controller exposure; event-driven/trend-following candidate rather than low-risk core.
Phison NAND controller and custom storage solutions; high valuation depends on AI storage mix continuing to rise.

risk_flags: Do not chase long green candles far above the 50-day moving average.
Reduce high-beta exposure if memory ASPs turn down, gross margins fall for two quarters, inventory rises with worsening operating cash flow, or AI capex expectations are cut.
High memory prices may damage non-AI PC/phone demand.
Export controls, geopolitics, currency, and liquidity risks differ across U.S., Hong Kong, China A-share, Korea, and Taiwan markets.
Options should remain auxiliary and defined-risk; not a shortcut to force a double.
portfolio_guidance: Use staged buying rather than one-shot full allocation.
Core growth names can receive larger research priority than high-beta tactical names.
High-risk single names should not dominate the account.
Cash/short-duration assets are useful while the sector is technically hot.
